summary: Pipeline board re-renders all cards on every keystroke, scroll, or presence tick because PipelineProvider's context value churns and every ContentCard reads its props through an unmemoized provider; on top of that the dashboard recomputes 7+ filter passes over the entire card list on every render, the Studio polls jobs every 3s without using the realtime channel already open, and the calendar performs O(N*D) filtering across cards per day-cell.

findings.title: PipelineProvider context value churns on every minor state change, re-rendering every consumer (board + dashboard + every card)
findings.impact: Each time the user opens the drawer, types in the kickback modal, drags a card, or any selectedCard mutation lands, every consumer re-renders. Components reading only `cards` re-run when `isDrawerOpen` flips. For a 100-card board, drag-end alone produces ~3-5 full re-renders of every <ContentCard/>. Estimated 30-80ms of wasted React work per interaction on a mid-tier laptop, far worse on mobile.
findings.measurement_idea: React DevTools Profiler — record a drag, count <ContentCard> commits. Should drop from N=cards-count down to ~2 (source + dest column) after split.
findings.fix: Split the context into two: PipelineDataContext = { cards, isLoading, workspaceId } and PipelineActionsContext = { selectedCard, drawer flags, all callbacks }. Actions context is stable (only callbacks), data context only invalidates when cards change. Or expose `useCards()` selectors via `use-context-selector` (already a tiny dep) so each <ContentCard> only re-renders when its own card row mutates.

findings.title: DashboardPage runs 7 .filter() passes over cards on every render with no memoization
findings.impact: 12+ full passes over the card array per render. With 100 cards = 1200 comparisons per render. The dashboard also triggers `AnimatedCounter` / `AnimatedBar` which re-renders many times per second during entry animation, multiplying the cost. Each presence tick or realtime UPDATE invalidates the whole dashboard.
findings.measurement_idea: console.time('dashboard') around the entire render. Compare counts shown in React Profiler before/after wrapping in `useMemo`.

findings.title: CalendarPage `getCardsForDay` filters all cards N times per render (one per day-cell), inside the render body
findings.impact: For a typical month (31 days + 6 leading blanks) with 200 cards = ~31 * 200 = 6,200 array operations per render. Re-runs whenever any card mutates, selectedId changes, or expandedCells toggles. Same identical anti-pattern in dashboard-page.tsx MiniCalendar (line 302).
findings.measurement_idea: Add console.time inside the day-cell map; flip a card stage and observe time. Should drop from ~10ms to <0.5ms after grouping.

findings.title: Studio job polling does redundant work — runs every 3s for every generating row, doesn't share a single tick
findings.impact: Effect depends on `rows`, so every realtime UPDATE on content_plan_rows tears down the interval and starts a new one. Polls AI route every 3s even though the realtime channel on line 165 already streams job/row updates from the same table. With 5 generating rows = 5 sequential HTTP calls per tick = a stuttering 3s loop. Each call is a service-role admin query against ai_generation_jobs.
findings.measurement_idea: Network tab — count `/api/ai/jobs/*` calls per minute during a 5-row batch. Should drop to 0 after wiring realtime job updates.
findings.fix: (a) Use the existing `content_plan_rows` realtime subscription — server-side mark the row status='generated' before signalling. (b) Or subscribe to `ai_generation_jobs` and react to status changes. (c) If polling must stay, use a single useRef'd interval and a stable jobIdsRef so the interval isn't recreated on every rows/jobIdByRow update.

findings.title: PipelineProvider creates a brand-new realtime channel whenever workspaceId changes (and workspaceId is set asynchronously after first paint)
findings.impact: On first load workspaceId starts as the placeholder UUID, then flips to the provisioned UUID after the /api/workspace/provision round-trip. That tears down + re-subscribes a fresh channel — a full WS handshake + presence resync. Doing it on first paint adds ~150-300ms of websocket churn and briefly delays first realtime events.
findings.measurement_idea: Chrome WS panel — observe the `posts-realtime-*` subscribe/unsubscribe pair on initial load. Should be one subscribe, no unsubscribe.
findings.fix: Wait for workspaceId to resolve before mounting the realtime effect: gate on `workspaceId !== '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'` OR move the entire subscription into a `useEffect` that runs after `provisionResult?.workspaceId` arrives. The dep array should also use the actual `workspaceId` (not `workspaceIdRef.current` read once), or just `[useSupabase, workspaceId]` after confirming the placeholder gate.

findings.title: Every <ContentCard> recomputes derived state (publishState/verifiedPlatforms/urgent/overdue) on every parent re-render, with no React.memo wrapping the component
findings.impact: Combined with the unsplit context (PERF-001), every card recomputes 6 derived values plus a Set construction on every keystroke anywhere in the app. For 100 cards = 600+ redundant ops per re-render. `verifiedPlatforms` particularly because of the Set + filter + map chain.
findings.measurement_idea: React Profiler 'Why did this render?' on a single <ContentCard> — should show 'props changed' only when the card row mutates, not on every parent update.
findings.fix: Wrap export with React.memo and a custom comparator that only checks card identity (`prev.card === next.card && prev.stageColor === next.stageColor`). The dnd-kit hook is fine with memo; useSortable identifies by id. Then derive `urgent`/`overdue`/`verifiedPlatforms` lazily inside a useMemo keyed on the specific card fields.

findings.title: Two settings panels poll the publish queue every 30s via a full SELECT *
findings.impact: While the Settings page is open, two intervals (line 1178 and line 1501) fire every 30s, each pulling 50 rows with all columns. Even when the user has left the queue tab. Not a runtime crisis (read-only, 50 rows) but it keeps the websocket warm + DB row-cache hot for nothing, and you have realtime available on the underlying tables.
findings.measurement_idea: Open Settings, leave tab idle 5 min, count requests to `v_publish_queue` in network tab. Should be 0 outside the user-initiated refresh after fix.
findings.fix: Gate polling on `activeTab === 'admin'` or whichever tab actually renders the queue. Better: subscribe to realtime postgres_changes on `publish_jobs` and re-fetch only when state changes. Reduce select to only the columns actually rendered.

findings.title: is a plain — no width/height, no lazy loading, no responsive sizes, no Next/Image
findings.impact: Card thumbnails, dashboard avatars, media library grid, lightbox — all use this. No `loading="lazy"`, no intrinsic dimensions = CLS on every list. A media library page with 100+ images hits the network all at once, hard. Big LCP regression vs Next/Image with sizes+priority+remotePatterns.
findings.measurement_idea: Lighthouse — LCP, CLS, Total Bytes. Open media library, count initial image requests in network tab (should drop ~80% once lazy loading kicks in).
findings.fix: Step 1 (zero-effort): add `loading="lazy" decoding="async"` default attrs in RawImage. Step 2 (real fix): replace with next/image. Configure next.config.js images.remotePatterns for Supabase storage + Google Drive. Use `priority` on first 5 thumbnails of the kanban board (or detect LCP image dynamically) and on dashboard hero. Add `sizes` so the browser picks the right srcset slot.

findings.title: Sidebar checks Studio access by fetching `/api/ai/studio/access` on every mount, even though provisionResult is already in AuthContext
findings.impact: Every authenticated render of the app fires an extra HTTP round-trip + a `supabase.auth.getSession()` (which can refresh the token). On top of `/api/workspace/provision` it's the second sequential auth-gated network call before the first card paints. StudioPage on line 113-127 also calls the same endpoint a second time. Double-fetched on Studio nav. Token is already in AuthContext.accessToken.
findings.measurement_idea: Network waterfall on cold load — count auth-gated requests before pipeline data arrives. Should drop from 3 to 1.
findings.fix: (a) Reuse `accessToken` from useAuth instead of re-fetching the session. (b) Make `/api/ai/studio/access` return the value as part of `/api/workspace/provision` so it's a single call. (c) Cache the result in AuthContext, share with both Sidebar + StudioPage.

findings.title: AuthProvider's onAuthStateChange handler re-fetches team_members on every session refresh and overwrites currentUser
findings.impact: Supabase fires onAuthStateChange on TOKEN_REFRESHED (~every hour) and INITIAL_SESSION on every tab focus in some configurations. Each fire resets currentUser to bare profile, then re-enriches from DB — causing two re-renders of every component that reads useAuth + the avatar to flicker. Also re-issues the team_members query.
findings.measurement_idea: Console.log inside the handler with the event name. Leave tab idle for an hour, watch the chain. Profile commits should drop after fix.
findings.fix: Switch on `_event`: ignore TOKEN_REFRESHED (just update accessToken without touching currentUser). Only re-enrich on SIGNED_IN or USER_UPDATED. Or memoize the enrich query result against email and only refetch if the email differs.

findings.title: PresenceProvider summary refresh fetches the full v_user_presence_summary view every 60s with `select('*')`
findings.impact: Once per minute every authenticated tab pulls the full presence summary. Cost scales with team size. The view's `best_known_seen` is the only field actually consumed by PresenceLabel; the row also has 9 other columns. Each fetch is ~5-15kb depending on team size and re-sets `summaryMap` (causing the context value to invalidate, which re-renders every PresenceLabel).
findings.measurement_idea: Network tab — observe v_user_presence_summary requests; compare payload before/after column trimming.
findings.fix: (a) Reduce select to just the fields PresenceLabel reads (`email, presence_last_seen, audit_last, auth_last_sign_in, best_known_seen`). (b) Stretch the cadence to 5 minutes — UI shows relative time so freshness within 5 min is plenty. (c) Pause the interval when document.hidden is true. (d) Memoize summaryMap by reference if nothing changed (deep-equality check on prev result hash).

findings.title: KanbanBoard sorts every column on every render via `sortForColumn(cards.filter(...))` inside a useMemo that depends on the whole `cards` array
findings.impact: Five .filter() passes + five .sort() per cards change. For 200 cards = 1000+ comparisons + 5 O(n log n) sorts. Mostly fine, BUT `sortForColumn` itself does `[...cards].sort(...)` which clones the array even though the input is already a fresh filtered array. Wasted clone per column.
findings.measurement_idea: console.time wrapping the memo callback. Compare per-column timings before/after grouping.
findings.fix: Group once: walk `cards` in a single loop, push into a `Record<stage, []>`, filter `posted` after grouping, then call `colCards.sort(...)` (mutating the already-fresh array, no clone). Net: 1 pass + 5 sorts.

findings.title: moveCard fires 2 sequential network calls (rest+notifications) where 1 round-trip + bg notify would do
findings.impact: Drop-to-approved takes the full chain (DB update → publish-job insert → email). Until createPublishJob resolves the rollback path is delayed. Average n8n cold-start is ~200-500ms; sequential awaits compound. The notifications fetch then has its own session refetch instead of using the cached token.
findings.measurement_idea: Drag card to approved_scheduled, count visible lag from drop to drawer enabling. Should drop ~300-500ms after parallelizing.
findings.fix: Parallelize: Promise.all([supabase update, createPublishJob, fetchNotify]). Surface a single rollback path if any leg fails. Cache `accessToken` in a ref instead of calling `supabase.auth.getSession()` every notify.

findings.title: All Supabase mutations call `supabase.auth.getSession()` again to grab the access token instead of reading from AuthContext
findings.impact: Each call hits the auth client's storage adapter, parses JWT, and (on slow tabs) can trigger a refresh round-trip. Per user action that means ~5-15ms of extra setup, plus a chance of token refresh while the user is mid-drag. The same `accessToken` is already in useAuth().
findings.measurement_idea: console.time('getSession') around a sample call. Multiply by call-site count per common interaction.
findings.fix: Read `accessToken` from `useAuth()` in callers that already have access. For utility functions, accept the token as a parameter so calling components inject it. The middleware-style 'always re-fetch' pattern is unnecessary for client-side already-signed-in flows.

findings.title: TeamProvider subscribes to realtime + reads team_members + writes localStorage backup on every team update
findings.impact: Every realtime team update (presence dot status change is NOT this, but team edits are) serialises the entire members array to localStorage. With 50 members this is ~20kb of JSON.stringify on the main thread. Combined with PERF-010 (auth state changes refetch team_members), the chain can fire on every token refresh.
findings.measurement_idea: Performance tab → Long Tasks during a team edit. Should not show a >5ms localStorage write after debouncing.
findings.fix: Debounce the localStorage write 500-1000ms — if `members` flips often within that window, only write the latest. Or drop localStorage backup entirely; the DB is authoritative.

findings.title: Studio's generate-batch route does sequential serial rate-limit consumes + serial DB inserts inside a for-loop
findings.impact: For a 14-row bulk generate: 14 sequential rate-limit checks + 14 sequential job inserts + 14 sequential row updates = 42 sequential round-trips. On a Vercel function with 100-200ms cold-start to Supabase that's 4-8 seconds of dead time before the response. The maxDuration may even tip.
findings.measurement_idea: POST a 14-row batch, measure response time. Should drop from ~5s to ~1s after batching.
findings.fix: (a) Rate-limit: consume N tokens in one call (extend `consume` to accept a `tokens` arg, or call it once with `tokens = ids.length`). (b) Bulk insert: build an array of job rows and `sb.from('ai_generation_jobs').insert(jobRows).select('id, plan_row_id')` in one round-trip. (c) Bulk update row statuses via `.in('id', rowIds)`. Net: 3 round-trips instead of 42.

findings.title: MediaPage's `cards.find` runs N times per asset render (O(N*M) usage lookup inside the map)
findings.impact: Each asset row calls `cards.find()` once per `usedIn` id. With 200 cards and 200 media rows × 2 usedIn ids each = 80,000 comparisons per render. Re-runs on every search keystroke (which mutates filteredMedia by reference even when the visible subset is the same).
findings.measurement_idea: Type a keystroke in the media search input. Profiler should show MediaPage rendering at < 8ms after fix vs current ~30-60ms.
findings.fix: Build a cards-by-id Map once via useMemo on `cards`: `const cardsById = useMemo(() => new Map(cards.map(c => [c.id, c])), [cards])`. `getUsageInfo` then does `asset.usedIn.map(id => cardsById.get(id)).filter(Boolean)`.

findings.title: lucide-react imports are tree-shakable but components import many icons each, no bundle splitting
findings.evidence:
import { X, Image as ImageIcon, Film, Layers, PlayCircle, Upload, FileVideo, Plus, CheckSquare, FileText, Link2, MessageSquare, FolderOpen } from 'lucide-react';
// dashboard-page imports 18, settings-page even more, app-shell another 12
findings.impact: lucide-react ships ESM and Next 16 does tree-shake, so this is bounded. But the `create-post-modal` is rendered always (mounted with `open=false`), pulling 13 icons into initial bundle. Same with kickback-modal, revision-modal. Each icon is ~1-3kb minified.
findings.measurement_idea: `npm run build` then inspect `.next/analyze` or run @next/bundle-analyzer. Look at lucide-react chunk sizes per route.
findings.fix: Dynamic-import the modals (`const CreatePostModal = dynamic(() => import('./create-post-modal').then(m => ({ default: m.CreatePostModal })), { ssr: false })`). They're only used on user action — no need to ship in the critical path.

findings.title: Service worker registers via inline script on every page load with no version check
findings.evidence:
<script
  dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
    __html: `if("serviceWorker"in navigator){navigator.serviceWorker.register("/sw.js")}`,
  }}
/>
findings.impact: register() is idempotent so it's mostly cheap, but the registration call still does an HTTP check for sw.js (or 304). Bigger problem: no scope, no update strategy — once installed, you're stuck with whatever the old sw.js cached. Not a perf hotpath but easy to mis-configure later.
findings.measurement_idea: Application tab → Service Workers; check the registration timing entry on a fresh hard-reload.
findings.fix: Move registration into a small client component that runs once after hydration with idle callback: `requestIdleCallback(() => navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js', { updateViaCache: 'none' }))`. Adds a version tag in sw.js so it auto-self-updates.

findings.title: DashboardPage `mounted = true` literal is meant to guard against SSR but is a no-op + leaks AnimatedCounter into every render
findings.impact: Originally this was likely a `useState(false)` flipped in useEffect to defer animations until after hydration. It's now a constant `true`, which means AnimatedCounter/AnimatedBar run on the very first render with no chance to settle. Small visual flicker + extra RAF work on cold load. Not catastrophic.
findings.measurement_idea: Performance tab on cold load — look for layout shifts and frame drops during dashboard mount.
findings.fix: Either restore the gate (`useState(false)` + `useEffect(() => setMounted(true), [])`) and animate after hydration, or remove the `mounted &&` checks entirely if animations on first paint are fine.
